title: Queue Task
description: One unit of work in the git-backed file queue. The orchestrator emits these from a plan; a tier-pinned worker claims one, runs it in a FRESH model session, writes a result + any decisions, then moves on. The file's directory lane (.tasks/queued|active|paused|done|failed) is the authoritative claim state; the `status` field mirrors it for readability.
$comment: EXPANSION JOINTS — `tier`, `status`, and `done.verify.kind` are the fields most likely to grow. additionalProperties:false enforces the 'version the contract' discipline shared with the plan and decision schemas. `source` yields the exact `provenance` block a decision record needs, so work and its decisions stay linked.

id.description: Globally unique. Convention: the plan-path composite, e.g. 'PLAN-031/PH-2/T-2'. Unique across plans, and human-traceable.

tier.description: Which worker pool picks this up. Immutable once queued — this is the cost lever, set by the plan's routing.

status.description: Mirrors the directory lane. `paused_for_research` = a halt decision point is open; `paused_for_human` = escalated.

source.description: Where this task came from in the plan. Copied verbatim into the `provenance` of any decision the worker emits.

goal.description: One scoped, verifiable outcome. If it needs an 'and', it is probably two tasks.

repo_state.description: Git ref the worker checks out before starting, e.g. a commit SHA or branch.

grounding.description: Prior decision IDs the worker reads before starting — precedent, not first principles.

spec_excerpt.description: Lean slice of the spec relevant to this task; link to the full spec rather than inlining it.

constraints.description: Hard limits. The worker stops and fails the task rather than violate one.

depends_on.description: Other queue task IDs that must reach `done` before this one is claimable.

done.description: Same shape as the plan's definition-of-done. `verify` is the machine exit condition the worker checks AND the evidence a decision cites — one source of truth.

decision_points.description: Known choice points carried over from the plan. `halt: true` means pause and emit an ADR before proceeding.

budget.description: Ceilings the worker passes to its rabbit-guard tripwires. Same numbers the PostToolUse guard enforces.

claim.description: Set atomically when a worker claims the task (move queued -> active + commit + push).

summary.description: 2-3 sentences. A handoff digest, never a transcript.

unblocks.description: Queue task IDs whose dependencies this satisfies.

blocker.description: Queue task ID of an unresolved dependency, if that is the cause.

attempts.description: Reassignment count. Past a max (e.g. 3) the worker escalates to a human instead of retrying.

created_by.description: Orchestrator model id that enqueued this, e.g. 'claude-fable-5'.

model_tier.$comment: EXPANSION JOINT — abstract tier, mapped to a current model id in one central place so tasks don't rot when versions change.
